mapping.a_element: probe D7-brane in the bulk background
mapping.b_element: test D-dimensional brane interacting with the bulk black hole
N-dimensional static spherically symmetric bulk black hole

mapping.a_element: brane embedding fixed by extremizing the worldvolume (DBI) action
mapping.b_element: brane embedding fixed by extremizing the test-brane (Nambu-Goto) action

mapping.a_element: effective horizon induced on the D7-brane worldvolume
mapping.b_element: horizon appearing in the brane's induced geometry when it crosses the bulk horizon

brief_justification: The two share only the arena and objects (a probe/test brane extremizing its worldvolume action in a black-hole background, with an induced worldvolume horizon), not the governing process each analyzes. Paper A's machinery is holographic linear-response transport, extracting a conductivity tensor and drag from an induced current via regularity at the worldvolume horizon; Paper B's machinery is critical-collapse analysis, Choptuik-type self-similar scaling at the threshold of a merger phase transition. There is no counterpart in A for B's critical threshold/self-similarity, and none in B for A's conductivity/current, so the driving methods differ.
